hook.text: A wealthy Black woman and a poor white man face different barriers. Neither race nor class alone explains their lives.

buildup.text: Kimberlé Crenshaw coined intersectionality in 1989 to describe how race, gender, and class overlap.

discovery.text: A Black woman's experience isn't just racism plus sexism. The combination creates unique disadvantages neither captures.

twist.text: Early feminism centered white women. Early civil rights centered Black men. Intersectionality filled the gap.

climax.text: Stratification is never one-dimensional. People occupy multiple positions that combine in complex, specific ways.

punchline.text: Inequality isn't one ladder. It's a web of overlapping ones.

quiz.question: What does intersectionality describe?
options: How race, gender, and class overlap to create unique experiences
The intersection of two academic disciplines
